title: PhIP Resolver Metadata Document
description: Schema for the resolver metadata document at /.well-known/phip/meta defined in Section 12.7. This document describes the resolver's protocol version, supported operations, conformance class, and federation state (root key, delegations, successor, mirror URLs).

authority.description: DNS-like authority name this resolver serves.

namespaces.description: Namespaces this resolver will accept CREATEs into.

items.description: Schema namespace identifier, optionally with @-prefixed semver (e.g. 'phip:mechanical@1.2'). Bare names mean 'latest supported'.

query_capabilities.description: Optional map describing supported filter operators, glob syntax, sort orders. Schema is open-ended.

root_key.description: PhIP URI of this authority's root key (Section 4.6.1). Used as the trust anchor for authority-transfer verification.

mirror_urls.description: URLs of read-only mirrors hosting frozen snapshots of this authority's records (Section 4.6.5).

successor.description: Present iff this authority has been transferred (Section 4.6.2). Clients SHOULD redirect subsequent requests to the successor.

authority.description: DNS-like authority name of the successor.

namespaces.description: Namespaces transferred to the successor. Use ['*'] to transfer all.

transfer_event_id.description: Event ID of the originating authority_transfer event. Required for the PhIP-Transfer-Event header.

conformance_class.description: Conformance class advertised by this resolver. Absence implies 'full' for v0.1 compatibility.

batch_max_events.description: Advisory maximum events per batch CREATE/PUSH. Resolvers MAY enforce a lower limit than the spec's 1000 cap.

mtls_required.description: If true, clients MUST present a TLS client certificate (Section 12.8.1).

mtls_ca_bundle_url.description: URL where the resolver publishes its CA bundle for client cert issuance.

namespace.description: Namespace being delegated. '*' delegates the entire authority.

prefix.description: Local-id prefix the delegation applies to. If omitted, applies to the entire namespace.

max_subdelegation.description: Maximum further delegation depth permitted under this entry. 0 = no sub-delegation. Absent = unlimited.
